title: gesneriales
intro: The rank Gesneriales is a former order of flowering plants, now considered an outdated classification. It was previously used to describe a group of plants that share certain characteristics, but the classification has since been revised. Families such as Orchidaceae, Euphorbiaceae, and Cyperaceae are not part of the Gesneriales order, as it is no longer a valid classification in modern taxonomy. The Gesneriales rank is no longer used in contemporary plant classification systems.

families.family_desc: Corsiaceae is a family of flowering plants, commonly known as the Corsia family, native to New Guinea.
